items.title: David Jones-Garcia, Spurs Sign Two-Way Deal
items.originalTitle: David Jones-Garcia, Spurs Sign Two-Way Deal
items.headlineZh: David Jones-Garcia，马刺签下双向
items.titleZh: David Jones-Garcia，马刺签下双向
items.summary:
David Jones-Garcia and the San Antonio Spurs have agreed to a two-way deal. Jones-Garcia went undrafted out of Memphis in 2024.

In 11 regular season games last season with the Spurs, Jones-Garcia averaged 2.9 points, 1.2 rebounds and 1.6 assists in 6.2 minutes.

Jones-Garcia also appeared in 16 G League games with the Austin Spurs, averaging 26.1 points, 6.8 rebounds, 3.5 assists and 1.9 steals in 32.7 minutes.

items.title: Bradley Beal Drawing Interest From Heat, Celtics, Clippers
items.originalTitle: Bradley Beal Drawing Interest From Heat, Celtics, Clippers
items.headlineZh: Bradley Beal Drawing 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Bradley Beal Drawing 相关消息更新
items.summary:
Veteran guard Bradley Beal is expected to sign with a playoff contender, after opting out of his contract with the LA Clippers. Beal is drawing interest from the Miami Heat and Boston Celtics. A return to the Clippers also can't be ruled out.

The Heat are looking to add depth and shooting after acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason. Beal has a longstanding friendship with Jayson Tatum, after both grew up in St. Louis, which has the Celtics in play.

Beal appeared in just six games for the Clippers last season, as he suffered a left hip fracture in early November. The veteran shooting guard joined LA after being waived-and-stretched by the Phoenix Suns. Beal is expected to be healthy and ready for training camp this fall.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，Bradley Beal Drawing Interest From 热火，凯尔特人，快船，目前仍属于消息或传闻阶段。

items.title: Mario Hezonja Drawing Interest From Multiple Teams
items.originalTitle: Mario Hezonja Drawing Interest From Multiple Teams
items.headlineZh: Mario Hezonja Drawing 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Mario Hezonja Drawing 相关消息更新
items.summary:
Real Madrid forward Mario Hezonja is drawing interest from multiple teams around the league, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.

Hezonja is widely expected to rejoin the NBA next season.

Hezonja is also drawing interest from teams who are also chasing LeBron James, with the 76ers, Cavs, and Heat the favorites to sign James.

Hezonja last played in the NBA for the Blazers during the 2019-20 season.
items.oneLineZh: Mario Hezonja Drawing 相关消息更新
originalTitles: Mario Hezonja Drawing Interest From Multiple Teams
Heat Say Link To LeBron James Press Conference Live Stream Posted By Mistake

Sharp was the G League Defensive Player of the Year last season, averaging 7.7 points, 7.3 rebounds and 3.9 blocks in 25.1 minutes over 39 games with the Texas Legends.

Jalen Slawson and the Indiana Pacers have agreed to a two-way deal. Slawson is represented by Aaron Turner and was the 54th overall pick in the 2023 NBA draft out of Furman by the Sacramento Kings.

Slawson appeared in 13 games with the Pacers last season while on a two-way, averaging 7.3 points, 4.4 rebounds and 2.8 assists in 23.9 minutes.

Lawson also appeared in 37 G League games, averaging 19.3 points, 7.3 rebounds, 5.4 assists, 1.3 steals and 2.3 blocks.

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg, Caleb Wilson, Cameron Boozer Headline 2026 NBA All-Sumer League Teams
items.originalTitle: Yaxel Lendeborg, Caleb Wilson, Cameron Boozer Headline 2026 NBA All-Sumer League Teams
items.headlineZh: 亚克塞尔·伦德伯格相关消息更新
items.titleZh: 亚克塞尔·伦德伯格相关消息更新
items.summary:
Memphis Grizzlies rookie Cameron Boozer, Milwaukee Bucks guard Brayden Burries, Golden State Warriors forward Yaxel Lendeborg, Cleveland Cavaliers guard Meleek Thomas and Chicago Bulls forward Caleb Wilson earned First Team honors.

Lendeborg was also Summer League MVP as he led the Warriors to the Summer League championship.

In six Vegas Summer League games, Boozer averaged 18.5 points, 8.0 rebounds, 3.7 assists and 1.8 steals in 29.5 minutes. Wilson appeared in four Summer League games, averaging 23.5 points, 7.2 rebounds, 1.8 assists, 1.2 steals and 2.5 blocks. Lendeborg averaged 14.9 points, 6.8 rebounds, 4.0 assists and 1.4 steals in 26.9 minutes.

The Second Team includes Memphis Grizzlies forward Cedric Coward, Brooklyn Nets guard Egor Demin, Phoenix Suns center Khaman Maluach, Charlotte Hornets forward Liam McNeeley and Minnesota Timberwolves guard Zyon Pullin.
items.dekZh: Lendeborg 场均14.9分，6.8篮板，4.0助攻和1.4抢断，出场26.9分钟。
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，亚克塞尔·伦德伯格、卡梅伦·布泽尔、Caleb Wilson、梅利克·托马斯等球员入选夏季联赛最佳阵容相关名单。原文重点是这些年轻球员在夏季联赛中的表现获得认可。

items.title: Cameron Boozer Says Versatility Sets Him Apart, Cites Knicks As Model
items.originalTitle: Cameron Boozer Says Versatility Sets Him Apart, Cites Knicks As Model
items.headlineZh: 卡梅伦·布泽尔 Says 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: 卡梅伦·布泽尔 Says 相关消息更新
items.summary:
Cameron Boozer said his ability to fill multiple roles on the floor is what sets him apart, pointing to the New York Knicks' championship run as a blueprint for the kind of versatile play teams now covet. The Memphis Grizzlies' first round draft pick spoke to The Athletic about his approach following a standout summer league performance.

"Versatility is the name of the game. That's what teams are looking for," said Boozer. "Teams want five guys who can make plays. Look at the Knicks this year. Everyone on that team was capable of doing more than one thing. Everyone was capable of playing on or off the basketball. That's what I feel like I can do best. I can screen. I can handle it. You can put me in a lot of different scenarios."

Boozer closely studied New York's title run, taking note of how the team shared the ball, moved cohesively on both ends of the floor, and relied on players capable of multiple skill sets rather than a single dominant scorer. Those observations shaped his own vision for how he wants to impact games as a professional.

That approach was on full display during a dominant 10-day stretch at summer league, where Boozer averaged 18.5 points, eight rebounds, 3.7 assists and 1.8 steals per game. His performance helped lead Memphis to the Las Vegas Summer League championship game, a narrow loss to the Golden State Warriors.

Selected third overall in the 2026 draft behind AJ Dybantsa and Darryn Peterson, Boozer is expected to be a day-one starter for the Grizzlies alongside Cedric Coward, last season's All-Rookie First Team selection. His combination of playmaking, physicality and positional flexibility as a 6-foot-10 forward has drawn comparisons to the multi-skilled roster construction that defined New York's championship approach.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，这篇文章围绕夏季联赛表现展开，重点是年轻球员、球队阵容和赛事结果带来的后续观察。
items.oneLineZh: 卡梅伦·布泽尔 Says 相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Cameron Boozer Says Versatility Sets Him Apart, Cites Knicks As Model

items.title: Walmart Heiress Nancy Walton Laurie Explores Las Vegas NBA Bid
items.originalTitle: Walmart Heiress Nancy Walton Laurie Explores Las Vegas NBA Bid
items.headlineZh: Walmart Heiress Nancy 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Walmart Heiress Nancy 相关消息更新
items.summary:
Nancy Walton Laurie and her husband, Bill Laurie, are considering a bid for an NBA expansion franchise in Las Vegas, according to three people familiar with the league's process, according to Sportico. The couple, worth $17.8 billion according to Forbes, live just outside Las Vegas in Henderson, Nevada.

Walton Laurie is the youngest daughter of Bud Walton, who co-founded Walmart with his brother Sam. She and her sister, Ann Walton Kroenke, split their father's Walmart stock following his death in 1995. Ann is married to Stan Kroenke, whose sports holdings include the NFL's Los Angeles Rams, the Premier League's Arsenal, the NBA's Denver Nuggets, the NHL's Colorado Avalanche and MLS's Colorado Rapids.

Monday marked the deadline for interested parties to submit details on capital sources and management plans for a potential Las Vegas franchise. NBA commissioner Adam Silver addressed the expansion process following the league's latest board of governors meeting.

"Multiple various, serious groups are in the process of presenting plans to our bankers," said Silver. "And those plans not only include who the potential owners would be, but their vision for what Las Vegas basketball could look like: where they would play, how they would present the game. And it's music to my ears."

Walton Laurie and her husband previously owned the NHL's St. Louis Blues before selling the team in 2006 to a group led by Dave Checketts for $150 million. This would not mark the couple's first pursuit of NBA ownership, having previously made unsuccessful attempts to acquire teams in Denver in 1999, Vancouver in 2000 and Charlotte in 2001. Bill Laurie came close to purchasing the Vancouver Grizzlies with plans to relocate the franchise to St. Louis, though the deal ultimately collapsed over relocation complications, and the team moved to Memphis ahead of the 2001-02 season.

Other known bidders for a Las Vegas expansion franchise include a group led by former Disney CEO Bob Iger and Thrive Capital founder Josh Kushner, as well as a bid from Vegas Golden Knights owner Bill Foley. A separate group known as the Las Vegas Jacks includes former Phoenix Suns owner Jerry Colangelo, former Turner Sports executive David Levy, American Century Investments CEO Jonathan Thomas and Prime Capital Financial chairman Scott Colangelo.

The depth of bidders for Las Vegas contrasts with Seattle where the interest has been softer than expected.
items.oneLineZh: Walmart Heiress Nancy 相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Walmart Heiress Nancy Walton Laurie Explores Las Vegas NBA Bid

items.title: Cam Christie Pushed Back Guarantee Date For Second Time
items.originalTitle: Cam Christie Pushed Back Guarantee Date For Second Time
items.headlineZh: Cam Christie Pushed 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Cam Christie Pushed 相关消息更新
items.summary:
LA Clippers guard Cam Christie has pushed back his contract guarantee date for a second time this offseason. Christie's $2.3 million contract with the Clippers is now set to become fully guaranteed on October 15. Given the proximity of that date to the start of the season, it's unlikely Christie's guarantee date will be pushed back again.

The deal for the third-year guard was originally set to guarantee in late-June. He agreed to push it back to July 20 earlier this offseason. Now, Christie has agreed to push back the decision until just before the start of the regular season.

The Clippers are likely trying to keep as much flexibility as possible with their roster. LA is still waiting for the results of the Kawhi Leonard investigation. A pending trade of Leonard to the Toronto Raptors has been put on an indefinite hold until the investigation is completed.
items.summaryZh: 洛杉矶快船后卫 Cam Christie 再次推迟了合同保证日期，现定于10月15日生效。Christie 与快船签订的 230 万美元合同原本在6月底保证，随后推迟至7月20日。快船希望在赛季开始前保持阵容灵活性，目前仍在等待 Kawhi Leonard 调查结果。
items.oneLineZh: Cam Christie Pushed 相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Cam Christie Pushed Back Guarantee Date For Second Time

items.title: Rich Paul Says LeBron James 'Won't Be Rushed' Into Making Decsion
items.originalTitle: Rich Paul Says LeBron James 'Won't Be Rushed' Into Making Decsion
items.headlineZh: Rich Paul Says 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Rich Paul Says 相关消息更新
items.summary:
On the latest episode of the Game Over podcast, Klutch Sports leader Rich Paul said that he doesn't know which team his friend and client LeBron James will play for next season. Paul made one thing clear about the process however, by saying that it won't be a snap decision.

"We're not going to be rushed," Paul said about James choosing his next team. "We're not going to be rushed. It's his choice to make and when he makes the choice, he'll make it."

Paul went on to say that James has all the information he needs to make his decision.

"There's been several people to call and say, 'Hey, Rich, is there anything more we should be doing? We could be doing?'" Paul said. "And my answer is, 'No. We don't need you to go above and beyond about anything. We understand it. It's clear. All the messages have been sent.'"

James said in recent public appearances that, "I won't hold you guys up too much longer." NBA commissioner Adam Silver weighed in to say that he hopes the NBA's all-time leading scorer decides soon, so that the league can finish next season's schedule.

"I saw Adam in New York and we discussed it," Paul said about speaking with Silver. "And like I said, it's still his choice. So, if he doesn't really know, you can't rush it. You can't rush it."
items.oneLineZh: Rich Paul Says 相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Rich Paul Says LeBron James 'Won't Be Rushed' Into Making Decsion

items.titleZh: Arthur Kaluma 与湖人达成双向合同
items.summary:
Arthur Kaluma and the Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way contract.

Kaluma went undrafted in 2025 out of Texas and spent the 2025-26 season in the G League with the South Bay Lakers. Kaluma averaged 13.8 points, 4.6 rebounds and 1.5 assists in 24.4 minutes across 40 games.

Kaluma appeared in seven Summer League games, averaging 17.3 points, 3.7 rebounds and 1.7 assists in 22.9 minutes while shooting 38.9 percent on three-pointers.

items.title: Mavericks Sign Jett Howard To Two-Way, Waive Tyler Smith
items.originalTitle: Mavericks Sign Jett Howard To Two-Way, Waive Tyler Smith
items.headlineZh: Sign Jett Howard 签约动向更新
items.titleZh: Sign Jett Howard 签约动向更新
items.summary:
The Dallas Mavericks have signed Jett Howard to a two-way contract. To create the roster spot, the Mavericks have waived Tyler Smith from his two-way contract.

Howard appeared in 55 games last season with the Orlando Magic, averaging 5.5 points and 1.6 rebounds while shooting 37.2 percent on three-pointers in 12.6 minutes. Howard was the 11th overall pick in the 2023 NBA draft out of Michigan. The Magic declined to pick up their fourth year option on Howard last October, which set him up for unrestricted free agency this offseason.

Smith appeared in 12 games last season with the Mavericks, averaging 4.7 points and 2.8 rebounds in 13.8 minutes. Smith was also on a two-way contract with the Houston Rockets last season but didn't appear in any games.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，Sign Jett Howard 的签约或合同动向成为焦点，原文围绕其自由市场前景展开。

items.title: Matisse Thybulle, Lakers Agree To One-Year, $3.3M Deal
items.originalTitle: Matisse Thybulle, Lakers Agree To One-Year, $3.3M Deal
items.headlineZh: Matisse Thybulle 与湖人达成一年，330 万美元合同
items.titleZh: Matisse Thybulle 与湖人达成一年，330 万美元合同
items.summary:
Matisse Thybulle and the Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a one-year, $3.3 million contract.

Thybulle is coming off his three-year, $33 million contract with the Portland Trail Blazers that was originally signed as a restricted free agent offer sheet with the Dallas Mavericks in 2023 that was matched. Luka Doncic was with the Mavericks when Thybulle signed the offer sheet.

Thybulle appeared in 30 games last season with the Blazers, averaging 5.8 points, 2.0 rebounds and 2.0 steals as one of the NBA's best wing defenders.

Thybulle missed most of the 2024-25 season due to right knee tendinopathy.

The Lakers currently have 16 players on guaranteed contracts for the 2026-27 season, which will necessitate additional roster maneuvers before the start of the regular season.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，Matisse Thybulle 与湖人达成一年，330 万美元合同，原文关注 Matisse Thybulle 的合同或自由市场动向。
items.oneLineZh: Matisse Thybulle 与湖人达成一年，330 万美元合同
items.displayTitle: Matisse Thybulle, Lakers Agree To One-Year, $3.3M Deal

items.title: Luka Doncic: I Think We Have A Really Good Team, Excited To Start New Season
items.originalTitle: Luka Doncic: I Think We Have A Really Good Team, Excited To Start New Season
items.headlineZh: 卢卡·东契奇相关消息更新
items.titleZh: 卢卡·东契奇相关消息更新
items.summary:
Luka Doncic spoke optimistically about the Los Angeles Lakers' roster during an interview over the weekend with the Golf Channel while he was attending the British Open.

The Lakers acquired Walker Kessler in a sign-and-trade with the Utah Jazz that cost the team two future first round picks outright and two first round swaps. The Lakers believe Kessler is the best two-way center Doncic has ever played with. The addition of Kessler and new rotation pieces such as Sandro Mamukelashvili, Quentin Grimes and Collin Sexton came at the expense of losing LeBron James.

"I think we have a really good team," Doncic said. "I'm very excited to start a new season. Obviously last season didn't end how we wanted, so I'm very excited for the new one."

When Doncic led the Dallas Mavericks to the 2024 Finals, he was paired with Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively splitting time at center.

Last August, Doncic signed a three-year, $165 million extension with the Lakers that contained a player option for 2028. Doncic can sign a four-year extension during the 2027 offseason.
items.oneLineZh: 卢卡·东契奇相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Luka Doncic: I Think We Have A Really Good Team, Excited To Start New Season

items.title: Trey Murphy Trade Talks Could Pick Up After LeBron James' Decision
items.originalTitle: Trey Murphy Trade Talks Could Pick Up After LeBron James' Decision
items.headlineZh: Trey Murphy Trade 交易影响继续发酵
items.titleZh: Trey Murphy Trade 交易影响继续发酵
items.summary:
The New Orleans Pelicans could revisit Trey Murphy trade scenarios after LeBron James decides which team he will play for this upcoming season. The Pelicans listened to trade offers around the draft but maintained a high asking price for Murphy.

"Chatter about a Trey Murphy trade, while lessened, hasn't vanished and is one of many potential pieces of league business that could resurface once LeBron James' next destination becomes clear," writes John Hollinger at The Athletic.

Murphy, who averaged 21.5 points, 5.7 rebounds and 3.8 assists across 66 games last season while shooting 37.9 percent from three-point range, is under contract through the 2029 offseason on a four-year, $112 million deal. He will earn $27 million next season.

The Pelicans selected Derik Queen and Jeremiah Fears in the first round last season and represent their youth movement. But the Pelicans also retain mid-career veterans such as Murphy, Zion Williamson, Dejounte Murray and Herb Jones.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM / Yahoo Sports 报道，Trey Murphy Trade 相关交易成为焦点，原文讨论这笔操作对球队阵容和后续走势的影响。
items.oneLineZh: Trey Murphy Trade 交易影响继续发酵
originalTitles: Trey Murphy Trade Talks Could Pick Up After LeBron James' Decision
items.displayTitle: Trey Murphy Trade Talks Could Pick Up After LeBron James' Decision

items.title: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks With Lakers Stall Over Contract
items.originalTitle: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks With Lakers Stall Over Contract
items.headlineZh: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks 签约动向更新
items.titleZh: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks 签约动向更新
items.summary:
Talks between the Los Angeles Lakers and free agent Jonathan Kuminga have stalled, with both sides far apart on contract terms, according to team sources. The Athletic has reported on the Lakers' interest in Kuminga since late June, but the team has shown little motivation to commit significant draft capital toward a sign-and-trade to complete a deal.

Despite the stalled momentum, Kuminga remains viewed as the top remaining upside play on the free agent market and could re-emerge as a Lakers target if his market stays cool. League sources indicated the Cleveland Cavaliers are the team most frequently connected to Kuminga aside from the Lakers. The Cavaliers are awaiting a decision from LeBron James before possibly pivoting to Kuminga.

The Atlanta Hawks previously declined their $24.3 million team option on Kuminga for the 2026-27 season. He played last season on a $22.5 million contract following a prolonged restricted free agency process with the Golden State Warriors.

The Lakers have also shown interest in free agent Matisse Thybulle, a top-tier defensive wing who has developed into an effective catch-and-shoot threat during his time with the Portland Trail Blazers.

Sources also indicated the Lakers continue monitoring the market for size at center. The team already acquired Walker Kessler via sign-and-trade and signed Kevon Looney to bolster that position, with Sandro Mamukelashvili also capable of playing center.
items.oneLineZh: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks 签约动向更新
items.displayTitle: Jonathan Kuminga's Talks With Lakers Stall Over Contract

items.title: Blazers, City Of Portland Remain Far Apart On Arena Funding Deal
items.originalTitle: Blazers, City Of Portland Remain Far Apart On Arena Funding Deal
items.headlineZh: City Of Portland 签约动向更新
items.titleZh: City Of Portland 签约动向更新
items.summary:
The Portland Trail Blazers and the city of Portland remain significantly apart over public funding for renovations to the Moda Center, according to a team source. The disagreement centers on a term sheet the city sent Friday outlining a proposed 20-year lease agreement.

"I think we are very far apart," said the source, one of two Blazers officials granted anonymity to discuss the situation.

Under the proposed terms, the Blazers would receive $600 million in public funding in exchange for paying $3 million annually toward a property tax offset, an amount that would increase over time. The deal would also require the team to hire union workers and accept caps on cost overruns.

"The term sheet is a non-starter," said the source. "I don't know why they sent it to us, because they know very little of the stuff we would agree to. They are playing politics at this point."

The Blazers' board is scheduled to meet Monday to determine next steps. The city plans to vote on the funding package Aug. 12, with an agreement needing to be finalized by Dec. 31 to preserve $365 million in committed state funding.

The team's lease at the Moda Center runs through 2030, adding uncertainty about whether new owner Tom Dundon might eventually consider relocating the franchise. Two high-ranking Blazers sources said Dundon has never raised that possibility.

"No one with the Blazers has ever said we want to move," said a team source. "We don't want that. We want to stay in Portland."

Tensions have grown between the organization and city officials, with some council members accusing the Blazers of withholding renovation details. City councilor Mitch Green criticized the team's approach.

"I think the Blazers have been behaving poorly," said Green. "They thought because they passed the state legislature that the deal was done. But we have a set of councilors who are asking tough questions."

The Blazers maintain their reluctance to share detailed plans stems from the early-stage nature of their renovation concepts and lingering frustration over a June threat from councilor Steve Novick to sue the team over the arena's condition.

"We don't even want to respond to the term sheet until they remove that gun to our head," said a source.

Renovation concepts under consideration include a new north-side atrium entrance, additional kitchens across the arena's three levels, and updated club-level seating.

Green said the council needs greater clarity before moving forward.

"We need to see a scope and scale of what this money is paying for," said Green. "You've got a city council that is not a rubber-stamp council... those are the conditions (the Blazers) face."

The Blazers believed they had reached an agreement in March after securing the state's $365 million commitment, contingent on the city releasing $120 million and Multnomah County contributing $88 million. A second Blazers source expressed frustration with how the situation has evolved.

"They have made this a bit of a political football," said the source. "They're asking for things that change the deal dramatically."

City spokesman Elliott Kozuch confirmed momentum existed in March but said formal council approval was always required beyond an initial understanding, adding that the Blazers have shared only high-level concepts so far.

A Blazers source said the team has begun studying other recent NBA arena deals and may push for improved terms going forward.

"The city's delay has given us time to study what a market deal is," said the source. "The $600 million deal that was supposed to be done six months ago was a way-below-market deal."

Green said he remains unmoved by the possibility of relocation.

"If he moves the team, it's because he thinks he can get a better deal," said Green. "Right now, it's a matter of $120 million. If Dundon came out today and said 'I'm gonna pick up the tab on the $120 [million]'... that would probably get us to the finish line tomorrow."
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，City Of Portland 的签约或合同动向成为焦点，原文围绕其自由市场前景展开。
items.oneLineZh: City Of Portland 签约动向更新
items.displayTitle: Blazers, City Of Portland Remain Far Apart On Arena Funding Deal

items.title: Warriors Win Summer League Championship; Yaxel Lendeborg Named MVP
items.originalTitle: Warriors Win Summer League Championship; Yaxel Lendeborg Named MVP
items.headlineZh: Win Summer League 相关消息更新
items.titleZh: Win Summer League 相关消息更新
items.summary:
The Golden State Warriors beat the Memphis Grizzlies 94-90 to win the Summer League championship on Sunday.

Yaxel Lendeborg, who finished with 21 points and 10 rebounds, was named the Most Valuable Player of the game.

"Coming into this program, I just wanted to prove that I'm a winner," said Lendeborg. "Doing stuff like this -- not caring about the stats and just caring about the outcome. I'm going to continue to push that into next season with the bigger Warriors."

The Warriors went 5-1 overall in the Summer League.

Cameron Boozer finished with 19 points and 8 rebounds.

"I've got to find a way to assert myself more," Boozer said. "At the end, I got two great looks, but they didn't fall. ... We lost, but this is why I love the game. It was high-intensity and back-and-forth."

Boozer averaged 18.5 points and 8.0 rebounds during Summer League, with the Grizzlies finishing 4-2.
items.summaryZh: 据 RealGM 报道，勇士击败灰熊夺得夏季联赛冠军，亚克塞尔·伦德伯格被评为本场最有价值球员。原文提到他拿到 21 分和 10 个篮板。
items.oneLineZh: Win Summer League 相关消息更新
items.displayTitle: Warriors Win Summer League Championship; Yaxel Lendeborg Named MVP

items.title: Jalen Duren, Pistons Have Made 'Modest' Progress In Contract Negotiations
items.originalTitle: Jalen Duren, Pistons Have Made 'Modest' Progress In Contract Negotiations
items.headlineZh: Jalen Duren 签约动向更新
items.titleZh: Jalen Duren 签约动向更新
items.summary:
The Detroit Pistons and Jalen Duren have made "modest" progress in their contract negotiations, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.

Pistons officials continue to reiterate that bringing back Duren is their top priority this offseason.

The team are also not interested in introducing any sign-and-trade scenarios involving Duren.

Duren could still accept the team's $9.6 million qualifying offer.

items.title: Hawks Acquire Lu Dort, Ryan Nembhard; Mavs Receive Zaccharie Risacher In Three-Team Trade With Thunder
items.originalTitle: Hawks Acquire Lu Dort, Ryan Nembhard; Mavs Receive Zaccharie Risacher In Three-Team Trade With Thunder
items.headlineZh: 雷霆将吕冈茨·多尔特送至老鹰
items.titleZh: 雷霆将吕冈茨·多尔特送至老鹰
items.summary:
The Oklahoma City Thunder have agreed to trade Luguentz Dort to the Atlanta Hawks as part of a three-team deal that also involves the Dallas Mavericks. Oklahoma City will receive three second-round picks, with former No. 1 overall pick Zaccharie Risacher going to the Mavericks, while the Hawks will also acquire Ryan Nembhard.

The move gives Oklahoma City seven second-round picks accumulated this offseason while pushing the team below the second luxury tax apron. The Thunder have now shed roughly $200 million in salary this summer through trades involving Dort, Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins.

Rather than decline Dort's $17.7 million team option to save money outright, Oklahoma City exercised it, then used the trade to acquire future assets, create a $17 million trade exception and position Dort for a long-term contract in Atlanta that wasn't financially feasible with the Thunder.

Atlanta now pairs Dort with Dyson Daniels and Nickeil Alexander-Walker on the wing, giving the Hawks a strong perimeter defensive group. The team will also carry more than $40 million in cap space entering next summer.

Dort was Oklahoma City's longest-tenured player, having joined the franchise as an undrafted two-way player in 2019 before earning multiple contract extensions. He averaged 8.3 points and 3.6 rebounds last season and has posted career averages of 11.6 points and 3.8 rebounds across seven seasons, all with the Thunder. He earned NBA All-Defensive First Team honors in 2025.

Dallas president Masai Ujiri and general manager Mike Schmitz had targeted Risacher throughout the offseason as part of an effort to build around the Mavericks' young core under new coach Dusty May. Risacher, 21, averaged 9.6 points and 3.8 rebounds in his second NBA season and finished as runner-up to Stephon Castle in Rookie of the Year voting following the 2024-25 campaign.

Nembhard, 23, averaged 6.6 points, 5.3 assists and 2.2 rebounds in his rookie season last year.
items.summaryZh: 雷霆在三方交易中将吕冈茨·多尔特送至老鹰，独行侠得到扎卡里·里萨谢，老鹰还得到瑞安·内姆哈德。
items.oneLineZh: 雷霆送走多尔特，独行侠得到里萨谢
